content.problemStatement:
Chef has two **distinct** positive integers AAA and BBB.

Chef wonders how many distinct values are possible for the expression gcd(A+X,B+X)\mathrm{gcd}(A+X,B+X)gcd(A+X,B+X), where XXX can take any non-negative integer value.

Help Chef find this value.

Here, gcd\mathrm{gcd}gcd stands for Greatest Common Divisor.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two distinct space separated integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the number of distinct values of the expression gcd(A+X,B+X)\mathrm{gcd}(A+X,B+X)gcd(A+X,B+X), where XXX can take any non-negative integer value.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Here gcd(1+X,2+X)=1\mathrm{gcd}(1+X, 2+X) = 1gcd(1+X,2+X)=1 no matter what value of XXX you choose.
Test case 2:

If we take X=0X = 0X=0, gcd(12,8)=4\mathrm{gcd}(12, 8) = 4gcd(12,8)=4.
If we take X=1X = 1X=1, gcd(13,9)=1\mathrm{gcd}(13, 9) = 1gcd(13,9)=1.
If we take X=2X = 2X=2, gcd(14,10)=2\mathrm{gcd}(14, 10) = 2gcd(14,10)=2.

It can be shown that no other value of gcd\mathrm{gcd}gcd is possible.
